channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: British PM Keir Starmer's job is in jeopardy after his party was trounced in elections
item.description: After a victory for the far right in municipal elections, Britain's center-left ruling party is in turmoil over whether to replace the unpopular prime minister, Keir Starmer — and with whom.
item.content:encoded: After a victory for the far right in municipal elections, Britain's center-left ruling party is in turmoil over whether to replace the unpopular prime minister, Keir Starmer — and with whom.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: Here's how the NYT crafts bestseller lists — and how authors try to game them
item.description: We go inside how The New York Times crafts the powerful bestseller lists, and the history of ways authors try to game the system — and sometimes succeed.
item.content:encoded: We go inside how The New York Times crafts the powerful bestseller lists, and the history of ways authors try to game the system — and sometimes succeed.
item.dc:creator: Alexi Horowitz-Ghazi

item.title: This week in science: Sunscreen from fish, art and aging, and a sustainable marimba
item.description: NPR's science podcast Short Wave brings us the stories of how future sunscreens might come from fish, how loving art may impact aging, and a student's quest to build a more sustainable marimba.
item.content:encoded: NPR's science podcast Short Wave brings us the stories of how future sunscreens might come from fish, how loving art may impact aging, and a student's quest to build a more sustainable marimba.
item.dc:creator: Regina G. Barber

item.title: New York Knicks fans are traveling well these days
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with The Athletic's James L. Edwards, about the economic incentives that have led New York Knicks fans to travel en masse to away games.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with The Athletic's James L. Edwards, about the economic incentives that have led New York Knicks fans to travel en masse to away games.
item.dc:creator: Jason Fuller

item.title: Some plants have unusual genetics, which can help them weather cataclysmic events
item.description: Unlike humans, many plants have more than two sets of chromosomes. This trait may help them adapt to environmental upheaval, such as climate change.
item.content:encoded: Unlike humans, many plants have more than two sets of chromosomes. This trait may help them adapt to environmental upheaval, such as climate change.
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: How AI is speeding new business creation, especially among Gen Z entrepreneurs
item.description: Entrepreneurs are turning to AI to speed the creation of new businesses, with Gen Z leading the way. That's according to a new report from the payroll software firm Gusto.
item.content:encoded: Entrepreneurs are turning to AI to speed the creation of new businesses, with Gen Z leading the way. That's according to a new report from the payroll software firm Gusto.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: Weezer and Beyoncé are among new National Recording Registry entries
item.description: Weezer, Beyoncé, The Go-Go's and music from the video game Doom join the National Recording Registry at the Library of Congress.
item.content:encoded: Weezer, Beyoncé, The Go-Go's and music from the video game Doom join the National Recording Registry at the Library of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Blair

item.title: The Supreme Court keeps abortion pill mifepristone available by telehealth
item.description: The Supreme Court ruled on Thursday that the abortion pill mifepristone can continue to be prescribed online or over the phone and sent through the mail.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court ruled on Thursday that the abortion pill mifepristone can continue to be prescribed online or over the phone and sent through the mail.

(Image credit: Jose Luis Magana)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: Trump and Xi make nice in China. What's it mean for the future?
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with China correspondent Jennifer Pak and White House correspondent Franco Ordonez about the relationship between President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with China correspondent Jennifer Pak and White House correspondent Franco Ordonez about the relationship between President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Pak

item.title: The DOJ's civil rights division is investigating gun rights violations
item.description: The Justice Department has a new special unit investigating violations of gun rights, and it's suing cities and states with gun control laws that may be vulnerable after recent Supreme Court rulings.
item.content:encoded: The Justice Department has a new special unit investigating violations of gun rights, and it's suing cities and states with gun control laws that may be vulnerable after recent Supreme Court rulings.
item.dc:creator: Martin Kaste
